name: quickbooks
description: Manage invoices, customers, and accounts in QuickBooks Online

envVars.name: QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID
envVars.description: Intuit OAuth Client ID (from your app's Keys & credentials page)

tools.name: List Invoices
tools.description: List recent invoices in a QuickBooks Online company

host.description: QuickBooks API host — quickbooks.api.intuit.com for production, sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com for sandbox companies

realmId.description: QuickBooks company ID (realm ID) shown in the OAuth callback and under Settings → Additional Info in QuickBooks

query.default: SELECT * FROM Invoice ORDERBY MetaData.LastUpdatedTime DESC MAXRESULTS 50

accept.description: Response format — keep application/json (QuickBooks defaults to XML)

historicalSummary.omitted: invoice line items, tax details, and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: List Customers
tools.description: List customers in a QuickBooks Online company

host.description: QuickBooks API host — quickbooks.api.intuit.com for production, sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com for sandbox companies

query.default: SELECT * FROM Customer ORDERBY MetaData.LastUpdatedTime DESC MAXRESULTS 50

accept.description: Response format — keep application/json (QuickBooks defaults to XML)

tools.name: List Accounts
tools.description: List the chart of accounts in a QuickBooks Online company

host.description: QuickBooks API host — quickbooks.api.intuit.com for production, sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com for sandbox companies

query.default: SELECT * FROM Account ORDERBY Name MAXRESULTS 100

accept.description: Response format — keep application/json (QuickBooks defaults to XML)

tools.name: Get Invoice
tools.description: Get a QuickBooks invoice with its line items

host.description: QuickBooks API host — quickbooks.api.intuit.com for production, sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com for sandbox companies

accept.description: Response format — keep application/json (QuickBooks defaults to XML)

tools.name: Create Invoice
tools.description: Create an invoice in a QuickBooks Online company

host.description: QuickBooks API host — quickbooks.api.intuit.com for production, sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com for sandbox companies

accept.description: Response format — keep application/json (QuickBooks defaults to XML)

tools.name: Run Query
tools.description: Run an arbitrary QuickBooks query against any entity (Invoice, Customer, Account, Payment, Bill, Item, ...)

host.description: QuickBooks API host — quickbooks.api.intuit.com for production, sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com for sandbox companies

query.description: QuickBooks SQL-like query, e.g. SELECT * FROM Payment WHERE TotalAmt > '100.00' MAXRESULTS 25

accept.description: Response format — keep application/json (QuickBooks defaults to XML)

prompts.title: Open invoices
prompts.prompt: List my QuickBooks invoices with an outstanding balance, sorted by due date.

prompts.title: Create an invoice
prompts.prompt: Create an invoice in QuickBooks for a customer with the line items I provide.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: QuickBooks Online OAuth Setup

steps.title: Create an Intuit developer account
steps.description: Sign up at https://developer.intuit.com/ — creating an app automatically provisions a QuickBooks Online sandbox company with sample data for testing.

steps.title: Create an app
steps.description: In the developer dashboard (https://developer.intuit.com/app/developer/dashboard), create an app for the QuickBooks Online and Payments platform with the com.intuit.quickbooks.accounting scope, and add your /api/auth/quickbooks/callback URL as a redirect URI.

steps.title: Copy credentials
steps.description: From the app's Keys & credentials page, copy the Client ID and Client Secret (Development keys work against the sandbox host; Production keys require app review). Set QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID and QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_SECRET in your .env.

steps.title: Note your realm ID
steps.description: The OAuth callback includes a realmId query parameter identifying the connected company — save it; every QuickBooks tool requires it as the realmId parameter. You can also find it under the gear icon → Additional Info in QuickBooks Online.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Invoices tool with your realmId (set host to sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com for a sandbox company).

notes: The token endpoint authenticates with HTTP Basic (client_id:client_secret base64-encoded)
Access tokens last 60 minutes; refresh tokens roll and remain valid for up to 100 days
The company realm ID arrives as a query parameter on the OAuth callback, not in the token response — it must be saved and passed to every tool
QuickBooks responds with XML unless the Accept: application/json header is sent (the tools set it by default)
Sandbox companies use the sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com host
